name: Brent Crude
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **BRENT COLLAPSE EXTENDED TO $107.96 ON MAY 6 (-1.7% 1D after May 5 -4% to $109.87) — DAY 5/5 OF SUB-$115 CHAIN COMPLETE; MAY 4 KINETIC POP FULLY UNWOUND.** Round-trip arc: May 1 $108.17 → May 4 $114.44 (kinetic restart, +5.80%) → May 5 $109.87 (Trump pause, -4%) → May 6 $107.96 (-1.7%). Cleanest cross-asset de-escalation re-pricing of the entire 67-day arc. Goldman Sachs CUT Q2 2026 Brent forecast to $90/bbl (from $99) and WTI to $87 (from $91), citing 'reduced geopolitical risk premium' and 'early signs of improving oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz' — sharp downgrade from April 29 'Brent could top $118.' JPMorgan's $150 overshoot warning substantially deferred. Cross-asset confirmation of de-escalation: VIX -5% to 17.38 (BELOW pre-May 4 baseline); S&P +0.55% Tue + +0.8% Wed; KOSPI +6.7% record on AI cycle; USD/INR pulled back to ₹95.13 from ₹95.36. **MARKET BIFURCATION:** Brent collapses on diplomatic-track pricing while tankers RALLY (Frontline +2.8%, BOAT +3.5%) on PERSISTENT physical chokepoint — Hormuz blockade NOT resolved (Trump pause is on US convoy only; blockade 'remains in full force'). The market is pricing two separate things: kinetic-war probability down + physical disruption persists. Forward path: $105-110 hold = pause-priced equilibrium; <$95 = full diplomatic resolution priced; >$115 = kinetic restart re-priced.

current.regime_shift_marker: May 5 — Trump pause = REVERSAL of May 4 kinetic re-pricing within 24h. Goldman Q2 forecast cut to $90 = institutional acknowledgment that kinetic-spike was mean-reverting. Hormuz blockade UNCHANGED but kinetic-war probability compressed.
current.dated_vs_june_backwardation: Backwardation eased as forward curve flattens with kinetic risk premium removal; physical-tightness signal residual but de-escalating
